rule: SUPPORT iff nulls differ (KS p<0.01) AND ours no-laxer; NARROW if the pairing null is DEGENERATE for our set-recall statistic (variance 0) -> pairing null inapplicable
interpretation: our claim object is the KEY (sign->value), a set-recall statistic that is INVARIANT to held<->candidate pairing; the Kessler/LexStat pairing-permutation null therefore does not apply to our statistic (variance 0). N4 narrows to: 'the appropriate null randomizes the key; the pairing null is not applicable', rather than the KS-difference framing.
